notes.issue: Multiple ACE route pages rely on a large navigation-heavy page-level block for route listings, fine structure, active-route counts, speed-gain claims, and weekday-rider scope. Writer pages use the block conservatively for ACE listing/program context and cite more specific meeting-document blocks where available. A future source-prep or semantic correction should split or replace the ACE page evidence with finer-grained blocks rather than hand-editing canonical JSONL.

These pages have thin direct route-specific evidence: LGA pages rely on 2011 route lists plus workshop comments, and Bee-Line pages rely on Fordham Road corridor route lists. Writer prose scopes the claims to source-time lists and avoids unsupported route-level ridership, headway, terminal, or schedule claims.
